Mental health conditions
Bipolar disorder
Bipolar disorder is a mental health condition where you have extreme mood changes. Medicines and talking therapy can help manage it.
Important: 
Get urgent help
Support is available if you or someone you know is having a mental health crisis or emergency, no matter what you're going through.
Find out where to get urgent help for mental health 
Symptoms of bipolar disorder 
The main symptom of bipolar disorder is extreme changes to your mood.
You sometimes have either:
high moods (mania or hypomania) – for example, feeling very happy, excited or energetic low moods (depression) – for example, feeling sad, tired or hopeless These moods usually last a few days or weeks at a time.
If you have bipolar disorder, you will usually have times where your mood is stable and you do not have any symptoms. This can last for weeks, months or years.
High mood (mania and hypomania) symptoms
High moods in bipolar disorder can be severe (called mania) or mild (called hypomania).
Symptoms of high mood include:
feeling very happy and excited feeling very irritable or aggressive having a lot of energy, feeling
